reasoning: The 4-week moving average of continued claims (CC4WSA) has shown strong upward momentum in the last four weeks, rising from 1,922,250 on Sept 20 to 1,947,500 on Oct 25, 2025. This momentum (roughly +8k per week) makes an increase for the week ending Nov 1 very likely. However, the series reached a similar peak of 1,949,000 in June/July 2025 before declining, suggesting the current trend is within a longer-term range or experiencing volatility. The year-over-year increase (from 1.87m in 2024 to 1.95m in 2025) suggests a softening labor market. Probabilities are set high for the immediate date due to momentum and the mechanical nature of the 4-week average, then trend toward 0.5 for the longer horizons to account for economic uncertainty and the possibility of the series remaining range-bound.
